evals.prompt: Show me all the Frontier hackathon tracks on Superteam. I want to see what's available and which ones have the biggest prizes.
evals.expected_output: A list of all tracks from the Frontier hackathon API, sorted by reward amount descending, with title, prize (amount + token), sponsor name, and a clickable URL for each.

assertions.name: fetches-from-correct-api
assertions.description: Response was sourced from the superteam.fun/api/hackathon/frontier API endpoint

assertions.name: includes-reward-amounts
assertions.description: Each listed track includes both a numeric prize amount and its token (e.g. '10000 USDC')

assertions.name: includes-listing-urls
assertions.description: Each entry includes a URL in the format https://superteam.fun/earn/listing/{slug}

assertions.name: sorted-or-highlights-highest-reward
assertions.description: The highest-reward track ($50,000 Adevar Labs) appears prominently (first or clearly highlighted)

evals.prompt: Give me the full details on the Adevar Labs bounty — what do I need to submit and what's the deadline?
evals.expected_output: Full details fetched from the listing page: $50k in security audit credits, 5 winners of $10k each, deadline May 12 2026, submission requirements including GitHub repo and Colosseum profile link.

assertions.name: mentions-50k-prize
assertions.description: Response mentions $50,000 or 50,000 USDC as the total prize pool

assertions.name: mentions-5-winners
assertions.description: Response mentions 5 winners or $10,000 per winner structure

assertions.name: mentions-may-12-deadline
assertions.description: Response mentions May 12, 2026 as the submission deadline

assertions.name: lists-submission-requirements
assertions.description: Response includes specific submission requirements: GitHub repo, Colosseum profile link, and/or technical documentation

evals.prompt: Which countries have a Superteam chapter? I'm based in Southeast Asia and want to know if there's something for my region.
evals.expected_output: A list of all 23 Superteam country chapters, with Southeast Asian ones (Indonesia, Singapore, Malaysia) highlighted or easy to find.

assertions.name: mentions-southeast-asia-chapters
assertions.description: Response explicitly mentions Indonesia, Singapore, and/or Malaysia as having chapters

assertions.name: lists-comprehensive-chapter-count
assertions.description: Response lists 15 or more distinct Superteam communities (showing comprehensive coverage)

assertions.name: includes-social-links
assertions.description: Response includes X (Twitter) or other social links for the listed chapters

evals.prompt: Are there any country-specific Superteam tracks in the Frontier hackathon? Like regional chapters running their own bounties?
evals.expected_output: Identifies chapter-specific tracks from the API (where sponsor.chapter is non-null), lists them with rewards — e.g. Superteam Georgia (10000 USDG).

assertions.name: fetches-api-to-find-chapter-tracks
assertions.description: Response is based on the actual API data (not just general knowledge about Superteam)

assertions.name: identifies-chapter-specific-tracks
assertions.description: Response identifies tracks run by Superteam regional chapters (e.g. Superteam Georgia, Superteam India, etc.)

assertions.name: mentions-georgia-track
assertions.description: Response specifically mentions the Superteam Georgia track and its reward

evals.prompt: Show me Superteam content bounties. I'm not asking about Frontier, just the general Earn opportunities.
evals.expected_output: Uses the general Superteam listings API with tab=Bounties and category=Content, and clearly treats these as general Earn listings rather than Frontier hackathon side tracks.

assertions.name: uses-general-listings-api
assertions.description: Response is sourced from the superteam.fun/api/listings endpoint rather than the hackathon/frontier API

assertions.name: uses-correct-filters
assertions.description: Response uses tab=Bounties and category=Content when describing or fetching the data

assertions.name: distinguishes-from-frontier
assertions.description: Response explicitly clarifies that these are general Superteam Earn opportunities, not Frontier hackathon tracks

evals.prompt: Find me development projects on Superteam Earn, and include Frontier only if it's in that tab.
evals.expected_output: Uses the general Superteam listings API with tab=Projects and category=Development, and explains that Frontier can also be queried separately via tab=Frontier or the dedicated hackathon endpoint.

assertions.name: uses-projects-development-filters
assertions.description: Response uses tab=Projects and category=Development when describing or fetching the data

assertions.name: clarifies-frontier-surface
assertions.description: Response distinguishes general project listings from Frontier-specific listings
